beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Full energy on Monday morning; Mia starts her week with a quick breakfast routine, taking advantage of the sunny forecast.

beats.activity: Drive from home to workplace in Interbay.
beats.reasoning: Regular Monday commute across the water; Mia drives north on I-405 to her real estate office, arriving before the Light Rail closure affects downtown traffic patterns.

beats.activity: Work on condo listings and client calls at the office.
beats.reasoning: Mia handles morning client intake and property showings; she's aware of the USA–Wales FIFA match at 11am but not a huge sports fan, so work continues through the morning without distraction.

beats.activity: Quick lunch errand: grab fresh ingredients at Pike Place Market.
beats.reasoning: Adventurous eater Mia detours downtown to source quality produce and proteins for the week; the sunny weather makes an outdoor market visit appealing, and she avoids the FIFA bars entirely.
Return commute after market run; Mia navigates around downtown to avoid Light Rail closure impacts and heavy midday traffic.

beats.activity: Lunch at desk with fresh market ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Mia enjoys the fresh ingredients she picked up; eating at the office keeps her afternoon schedule flexible for client meetings.

beats.activity: Afternoon condo showings and property consultations.
beats.reasoning: Mia conducts back-to-back client meetings and virtual tours; productive Monday afternoon in her real estate specialty.

beats.activity: Drive to food bank in South Seattle for volunteer shift.
beats.reasoning: Mia leaves the office early to honor her weekly food bank commitment; the sunny weather energizes her to help, and she heads to Ballard for the evening shift.

beats.activity: Volunteer at food bank: sorting, packing, and restocking shelves.
beats.reasoning: Mia dedicates her evening to food bank work, connecting with regular volunteers and fulfilling her community commitment; she values this meaningful work over passive leisure.
Evening commute back across the water; Mia heads home as the sun begins to set, wrapping up a full, purposeful Monday.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner and write handwritten letters to friends.
beats.reasoning: Mia unwinds at home with a home-cooked meal using market ingredients, then settles into her letter-writing hobby—a quiet, reflective way to end the day before bed.

diary:
Started the day with my usual routine—coffee while the sun was still soft over Kirkland. The drive to Interbay was smooth; I barely noticed the light rail closure since I'm always on the roads anyway. Morning flew by with listing calls and emails. Around noon I decided to duck down to Pike Place Market instead of my usual sandwich shop. The market was buzzing, probably because of the World Cup match starting at 11—I could hear bars downtown already filling up. I grabbed fresh salmon and vegetables, felt good to break up the fluorescent office air.

Afternoon showings went well. One young couple loved the Wallingford condo; I think they'll make an offer. After work I headed straight to the food bank in Ballard for my Monday shift. Sorting donations, talking with the other volunteers—there's something grounding about that work. Came home, made a simple dinner with what I'd picked up, and spent the evening writing letters. Real letters, with a pen. Feels rare these days.
thought_process: I was a bit scattered this morning—the World Cup buzz had me checking my phone more than usual, though I didn't watch the match. Decided the Pike Place detour would reset my energy for the afternoon, and it worked. The market always reminds me why I live here. Work felt productive but routine until those showings; that couple's genuine interest gave me momentum heading into evening. The volunteer shift was what I really needed today—something about restocking shelves and talking to people about real needs puts my real estate hustle in perspective. By evening I was grounded enough to slow down with handwritten letters instead of scrolling. The sunny weather probably helped my mood too; I felt less rushed, more present.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
